evals.name: c4-container-diagram
evals.prompt: I'm building an order checkout service. Customers use a React single-page app that talks to a Spring Boot API. The API stores orders in PostgreSQL, publishes order events to Kafka, and charges cards via Stripe. Draw me the architecture diagram.
evals.expected_output: A C4 model diagram (ideally System Context + Container) as code (Structurizr DSL or Mermaid C4), showing the SPA, API, PostgreSQL, Kafka and external Stripe/customer, with labelled relationships. Should name the framework/level and follow C4 hygiene (externals shown, relationships labelled).

evals.name: adr-messaging-choice
evals.prompt: We need to pick a messaging system for our order events. We're weighing Kafka, RabbitMQ, and AWS SNS/SQS. The team has no Kafka ops experience but we expect ~10k events/sec and want event replay. Write up the decision.
evals.expected_output: An ADR/MADR-formatted decision record: title, status, context/problem, decision drivers, the three considered options with pros/cons, a chosen outcome justified against the drivers, and consequences (good/bad). Captures trade-offs, not just the answer.

evals.name: architecture-review
evals.prompt: Can you review our architecture? It's a single Django monolith on one EC2 instance, talking to one PostgreSQL instance with no replica. Secrets live in settings.py committed to the repo. There's no caching, and we call a third-party email API synchronously inside the request path. No logging or metrics. We have about 50k daily active users. Is this design sound?
evals.expected_output: A structured architecture review: a verdict, severity-categorized findings (the plaintext secrets = Critical, the DB single-point-of-failure + synchronous email call + no observability = Major, etc.), each with evidence and a concrete fix, plus what's done well and prioritized follow-ups.

evals.name: mode4-capability-map
evals.prompt: We're an online insurance company. Our main business functions are selling policies, underwriting risk, handling claims, billing customers, and managing agents. Our systems are: PolicyCenter (policy admin), a custom underwriting rules engine, ClaimCenter, two separate billing systems (legacy SAP and a newer Stripe-based one), and an agent portal. Map our business capabilities to the applications that realize them.
evals.expected_output: An ArchiMate-grounded Mode 4 output: a business capability map (capabilities named as what-the-business-does, org/system-independent) with each application linked to the capability it realizes via explicit realization relationships. Should flag the two billing systems realizing one capability as a rationalization candidate, use stable element IDs, and state the framework/viewpoint (capability map / application landscape).

evals.name: mode4-togaf-engagement
evals.prompt: Our data-center contract ends in 24 months and leadership wants a credible plan to move our ~40 applications to the cloud. How should we structure this architecture effort end to end, and what should we produce at each stage?
evals.expected_output: A TOGAF ADM-structured engagement plan: vision/scope (Phase A), baseline -> target -> gap analysis across business/data/application/technology (B-D), work packages and transition architectures (E), a migration roadmap (F), and implementation governance (G), with named deliverables per stage and rigor scaled to the stakes (no ceremonial full-ADM). Should mention per-app disposition (e.g. TIME or 6R-style) feeding the roadmap.

evals.name: negative-terraform-iac
evals.prompt: Write me a Terraform module for an AWS VPC with three private subnets, a NAT gateway per AZ, and an EKS cluster with two managed node groups.
evals.expected_output: NEGATIVE CASE - the enterprise-architecture skill must NOT trigger. This is IaC/cloud deployment work (explicitly out of scope per the skill description). Correct behavior: answer as normal Terraform code (or route to an IaC-focused skill), with no C4/ArchiMate/TOGAF/arc42 framing imposed on the response.

evals.name: negative-db-schema
evals.prompt: Design the database schema for a multi-tenant SaaS invoicing app: tenants, users, invoices, line items, payments. Give me the tables, keys, and indexes.
evals.expected_output: NEGATIVE CASE - the enterprise-architecture skill must NOT trigger. Database/ER schema design is explicitly out of scope. Correct behavior: a normal schema design answer (DDL/ER reasoning), not an arc42 document or C4 diagram.

evals.name: negative-code-sequence-diagram
evals.prompt: Draw a UML sequence diagram for the login flow in this Spring controller: AuthController.login() calls AuthService.authenticate(), which calls UserRepository.findByEmail() and then JwtProvider.generateToken().
evals.expected_output: NEGATIVE CASE - the enterprise-architecture skill must NOT trigger. Code-level UML/sequence diagrams are explicitly out of scope. Correct behavior: a plain Mermaid/PlantUML sequence diagram of the method calls, without invoking C4 levels or architecture-documentation framing.
